mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect expanding supply capacity and real economic momentum, which historically leads to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO rose from 98.5 to 99.36 over Summer 2013
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC of INDPRO on forward 20-day returns is negative or the mean forward return in months with positive INDPRO change is not significantly above zero (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in housing starts above the important psychological 1,000,000 level signals a robust residential construction recovery, which historically boosts GDP and employment, supporting stock prices over a quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST averaged 891K in Aug 2013, up from 782K a year earlier
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative or flat (median <0) in the 6 months following a HOUST>1000 trigger, or hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) creates a virtuous cycle of rising incomes and spending, amplifying earnings growth expectations and delivering positive returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in both PAYEMS and RSAFS is positive
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS increased by 169K in Aug 2013; RSAFS increased 0.2% month-over-month
failure_condition: In quarters where both series post 3-month positive growth, forward 20-day returns are negative more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a low growth regime (GDPC1 annual growth below 2%), accelerating inflation (rising CPIAUCSL) raises stagflation concerns, reducing equity valuations as real discount rates rise and margins are squeezed.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 growth in 2013 Q2 was ~1.8% annualized, below 2%
fact:CPIAUCSL YoY was about 1.1% as of Aug 2013
failure_condition: In regimes with GDPC1 growth <2%, rising CPIAUCSL (3-month acceleration) is followed by positive forward 20-day returns in more than 50% of events.

mechanism_narrative: The persistence of the federal funds rate at the zero lower bound (0.08%) signals the Fed's commitment to accommodation, compressing discount rates and raising equity valuations as investors seek yield.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns during ZLB periods do not have a positive mean exceeding the risk-free rate, or the correlation of FEDFUNDS with forward returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll gains (exceeding 200,000) signal a labor market gaining traction, which feeds into consumer spending and business investment, historically leading to positive stock returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS change in Aug 2013 was 169K, below 200K
failure_condition: In months following a >200K PAYEMS print, forward 20-day returns are negative more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansions in industrial production and housing starts indicate broad-based cyclical strength, not limited to one sector, which substantially increases confidence in sustained recovery and boosts equity prices over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change positive for both INDPRO and HOUST
failure_condition: In quarters where both proxies rise, forward 60-day median return is not positive, or the signal's Sharpe ratio over multi-year periods is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index is below its long-run trend (approximated by 100), an increase in production signals that slack is being absorbed without creating bottlenecks, which is particularly positive for equities as it boosts margins without wage pressure.
failure_condition: In periods when INDPRO <100 and rising, forward 5-day returns are not positive with at least 55% win rate, or the average return is negative.
